name: EpicNPC
description: A forum-style, consumer-to-consumer marketplace for buying, selling and trading game accounts, in-game items and services (boosting, coaching, carries).

details.targetMarket: Gamers and semi-professional sellers who buy/sell/trade game accounts, currency, boosting services and coaching

items: Forum-style listing threads organized by game and tags
Category and tag pages for ~1000+ supported games (search by game)
User feedback and thread bumping to surface active offers
Trade Guardian (site-hosted escrow / middleman service) with tiered paid options
Public feedback/reputation system and paid ‘upgrades’/verified badges
Phone verification (Twilio) and ID/photo verification options for users
Paid account upgrades (Epic Member) to increase visibility and perks
Built-in Trade Guardian fee handling and deposit workflows
Large active forum community with dispute/reporting threads
Manual moderation and review for upgrades and verified status
Help center and extensive how-to threads (buying guide, trade guardian guides)

missingFeatures: No guaranteed chargeback/recovery insurance: Trade Guardian explicitly does not protect against payment chargebacks or account recalls, leaving buyers exposed for high-ticket purchases
No standardized service packages or SKU pricing: sellers list custom offers per thread which makes comparison and conversion harder for buyers seeking repeatable coaching or boosting packages
Limited identity/verification automation: verification relies on phone and manual ID review rather than a strong KYC/verified-vendor program with cryptographic identity or third-party attestations
Weak post-transaction protection and refunds: dispute resolution is manual and often only actionable when Trade Guardian is used, creating friction and risk for off-platform payments
No public seller analytics or performance dashboards to help buyers evaluate service quality beyond raw feedback counts
No documented API or B2B integrations for studios, platforms or payment partners to onboard verified pro sellers or offer in-platform protections

pricingPlans.name: Browse (free)
pricingPlans.note: Anyone can browse listings and contact sellers for free; creating sale threads requires registration and verification.

pricingPlans.name: Epic Member (upgrade)
pricingPlans.note: Paid user upgrade (visibility/perks/verified badge). Price has changed historically; current community threads reference $10/yr as the Epic Member price.

pricingPlans.name: Trade Guardian (escrow/middleman)
pricingPlans.note: Paid escrow/middleman service with variable fees (examples listed on site: flat + percentage fee structure). Protects against some scams but does not cover chargebacks or account recalls.

pricingEvidence.label: Member upgrade announcement (forum thread)
pricingEvidence.claim: Post explaining changes to paid upgrades and a cited Epic Member price of $10/yr for the main upgrade tier.

pricingEvidence.label: Trade Guardian page
pricingEvidence.claim: Explains Trade Guardian escrow/middleman options and fees (site lists flat + percentage fees for services).

companyEvidence.label: Twilio customer story
companyEvidence.claim: Describes EpicNPC as a US-based marketplace, cites ~495K members, ~300K monthly visitors and usage of Twilio Verify for phone authentication.

companyEvidence.label: EpicNPC Info Center (contact/about)
companyEvidence.claim: Site Info Center listing EpicNPC contact address in New Jersey and describing the marketplace/forum nature of the site.

companyEvidence.label: Crunchbase company page
companyEvidence.claim: Company listing on Crunchbase identifying EpicNPC Marketplace (no reliable public funding data listed).

reviewEvidence.label: Trustpilot reviews
reviewEvidence.claim: Multiple customer reviews citing slow/poor customer support and complaints about dispute handling when Trade Guardian is not used.

reviewEvidence.label: Reddit threads (community complaints)
reviewEvidence.claim: Community posts reporting scams, impersonation and skepticism about middlemen / Trade Guardian reliability.

featureEvidence.label: Trade Guardian info
featureEvidence.claim: Explains Trade Guardian services (free guidance vs paid middleman/escrow) and limitations (does not protect against chargebacks or account recalls).

featureEvidence.label: Buying & Selling Guide (help center)
featureEvidence.claim: Provides step-by-step guidance for buyers/sellers, recommended safety practices and advice to use Trade Guardian for risky transactions.

featureEvidence.label: Twilio customer story
featureEvidence.claim: Describes site verification (Twilio Verify), scale metrics and claim that the site supports ~1000+ games and large volumes of postings/sales.

strengths: High liquidity and category breadth: long-running forum with ~495K members and support for ~1000+ games, which provides supply and buyer depth for niche game items and accounts (Twilio case study).
Established community & moderation workflows: forum structure, reporting/dispute threads and a staffed Trade Guardian middleman program that can be used to reduce common scam vectors.
Low-friction discovery for niche items: forum thread model + tags/pinned posts makes it easy to find rare game-specific accounts or services that larger marketplaces don’t handle.
weaknesses: Insufficient payment protections: Trade Guardian explicitly does not cover chargebacks or account recalls, leaving buyers of high-value accounts exposed and reducing trust for expensive transactions.

Uneven trust signals: reputation is primarily feedback-based and paid upgrades can be used to boost visibility, which makes it possible for experienced scammers to appear legitimate and for new honest sellers to struggle for visibility.

Fragmented productization: services and coaching are listed as free-form threads (no standardized SKUs, recurring booking, or subscription options), which increases buyer friction for repeat or B2B purchases.
Customer support and dispute handling complaints: multiple Trustpilot and Reddit posts cite slow responses or unsatisfactory outcomes unless the paid Trade Guardian flow was used.
